item.title: Irish Protests Disrupt Roads Despite New Government Fuel Package
item.description: Drivers on Ireland’s busiest motorway still face delays even after a wave of fuel protest blockades deescalated over the weekend.

item.title: China’s 30-Year Bonds Gain Amid Bets on Issuance Duration Cut
item.description: China’s super-long government bonds pushed higher on Monday after traders bet Beijing may scale back the maturity on its special-debt issuance to ease supply pressure.

item.title: How Would Trump’s Blockade of the Strait of Hormuz Work?
item.description: US President Donald Trump’s plan for a full naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz threatens to deepen an already unprecedented crisis in one of the world’s most important energy-producing regions.

item.title: Zambia’s Planned Refinery to Rely on Oil Delivery by Road, Rail
item.description: Zambia will supply a refinery it’s planning to build and complete before 2028 with crude delivered initially by road, according to the energy ministry.

item.title: Fund Managers Seek Reporting Crackdown After Philippines Scandal
item.description: Money managers led by BNP Paribas Asset Management and Robeco Institutional Asset Management BV are putting pressure on the Philippine government to introduce stricter reporting standards for state-backed projects, following a corruption scandal tied to flood infrastructure.

item.title: China Starts Bankruptcy Liquidation of Shadow Bank Zhongzhi
item.description: A Beijing court has ordered the liquidation of Zhongzhi Enterprise Group Co. and its more than 300 affiliated companies, in a major step toward winding down one of China’s largest shadow banking conglomerates.

item.title: Ziad Daoud Explains How War with Iran Will Reshape the Gulf
item.description: From politics to petrodollars.

item.title: Odd Lots: Daoud on How Iran War Will Reshape the Gulf (Podcast)
item.description: Odd Lots: Daoud on How Iran War Will Reshape the Gulf (Podcast)【原文：Odd Lots: Daoud on How Iran War Will Reshape the Gulf (Podcast)】

item.title: Hong Kong Issues 53% More IPO Banker Licenses to Ease Shortage
item.description: Hong Kong issued 53% more permits for bankers specializing in initial public offerings last month, signaling a tentative recovery even as the regulator maintains a high bar for industry entry.

item.title: Ueda’s Speech Shows Rising Caution Without Clear Hints on Rate
item.description: Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda signaled a more cautious stance on raising interest rates by adjusting his language in one of his few remaining opportunities to send a message ahead of this month’s policy meeting.

item.title: Top Polish Bank PKO Speeds Up Hungary Branch Plan After Election
item.description: Poland’s largest lender PKO Bank Polski SA is speeding up its evaluation of opening a branch in Hungary after a pro-European opposition secured a landslide victory.

item.title: China’s TCL Is Said to Consider Stake Sale in India TV Business
item.description: TCL Electronics Holdings Ltd. is considering selling a stake in its Indian television manufacturing unit to local buyers to help grow the business, people familiar with the situation said.

item.title: Lufthansa Two-Day Pilot Strike Set to Cancel Hundreds of Flights
item.description: Deutsche Lufthansa AG faces another two-day pilot strike over pensions, threatening fresh travel disruption just days after a cabin crew walkout ended.

item.title: Wise on Track for Nasdaq Listing Next Month as Income Jumps 24%
item.description: Wise Plc said it’s on track to get its primary listing shifted from London to Nasdaq next month, as the money transfer company’s key fourth-quarter earnings figure beat analyst expectations.

item.title: Dolce & Gabbana Appoints Former Gucci Boss Cantino as Co-CEO
item.description: Dolce & Gabbana appointed former Gucci boss Stefano Cantino as co-chief executive officer following the departure of co-founder Stefano Gabbana as chairman.

item.title: Hexagon to Buy Baker Hughes’ Waygate For $1.45 Billion
item.description: Hexagon AB, a Swedish company that makes sensors and measurement technology, agreed to buy Waygate Technologies for about $1.45 billion in cash from Baker Hughes Co.

item.title: FTSE 100 Set to Fall as US-Iran Talks Fail
Toto Halts Bathroom Orders After Iran War Upends Supply Chain
item.description: Japanese toilet maker Toto Ltd. has suspended new orders for its prefabricated bathrooms due to a material shortage as the Iran war continues to squeeze the global oil supply chain.

item.title: Western Australia Mulls Own Diesel Reserve After War Disruptions
item.description: Western Australia is considering its own strategic stockpile of diesel with fuel paid for by the state after the Iran war led to shortages for key industries such as farming and mining.

item.title: Saudi Oil Sales to China to Halve as Hormuz Crisis Lifts Prices
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s crude sales to top importer China are set to halve next month as the war in the Middle East upends flows and lifts prices, according to traders familiar with the matter.

item.title: Are Australia’s Low Fuel Reserves Cause for Concern?
item.description: Despite being a major producer and exporter of energy, Australia relies heavily on imported refined fuel to power transport, agriculture and other parts of its economy, leaving it highly exposed to disruptions in global supply chains.

item.title: Franklin Templeton Taps Blue Owl’s Yamamoto as Japan Lead
item.description: Franklin Templeton’s credit management arm has appointed Takeshi Yamamoto as head of capital formation for Japan, according to a document seen by Bloomberg.

item.title: Oil May Rise to $150 If US Goes Ahead With Blockade, Onyx Says
item.description: Oil prices should be much higher than current levels if the US goes ahead with a planned naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, according to a veteran oil market executive.

item.title: Swiss Bank UBP Buying Gold Again, Forecasting $6,000 Year-End
item.description: Union Bancaire Privée is buying gold again after cutting a significant position in response to an Iran war-induced slump, saying it believes the long-term outlook remains intact.

item.title: Emerging Assets Slide as US Hormuz Blockade Saps Risk Sentiment
item.description: Emerging assets declined as escalating tensions in the Middle East following a breakdown in peace negotiations sapped risk sentiment.

item.title: China’s Clean Tech Firms Signal Windfall From Gulf Energy Shock
item.description: Chinese clean‑tech manufacturers are beginning to benefit from the supply crunch in the Persian Gulf, as rising oil and natural gas prices and a renewed emphasis on energy security boost demand for batteries and electric vehicles.

item.title: Malaysia Detains Two Tankers for Alleged Illegal Diesel Transfer
item.description: Malaysia’s maritime authorities detained two tankers and its crew members suspected of conducting an illegal ship-to-ship diesel transfer off Penang, in one of the latest crackdowns on illicit fuel activities in the country’s waters.

item.title: Whoever’s Stopping It, the Oil Doesn’t Get Through
item.description: The war and supply interruption will now likely escalate before they improve.

item.title: Taiwan Leader to Visit Eswatini in First Foreign Trip Since 2024
item.description: Taiwan’s President Lai Ching-te plans to visit the island’s last remaining diplomatic ally in Africa, in his first trip abroad since December 2024.

item.title: Tough Earnings Season Beckons as Iran War Hurts European Growth
item.description: As an earnings season clouded in geopolitical uncertainty gets underway, growth expectations for European companies might turn out to be far too ambitious.
